v.CNV: 这麦基洗德就是撒冷王，又是至高　神的祭司。亚伯拉罕杀败众王回来的时候，麦基洗德迎接他，并且给他祝福。
v.ESV: For this Melchizedek, king of Salem, priest of the Most High God, met Abraham returning from the slaughter of the kings and blessed him,
v.GAE: 이 멜기세덱은 살렘 왕이요 지극히 높으신 하나님의 제사장이라 여러 왕을 쳐서 죽이고 돌아오는 아브라함을 만나 복을 빈 자라
v.KJV: For this Melchisedec, king of Salem, priest of the most high God, who met Abraham returning from the slaughter of the kings, and blessed him;
v.NASB: For this Melchizedek, king of Salem, priest of the Most High God, who met Abraham as he was returning from the slaughter of the kings and blessed him,
v.NIV: This Melchizedek was king of Salem and priest of God Most High. He met Abraham returning from the defeat of the kings and blessed him,
v.NRSV: This “King Melchizedek of Salem, priest of the Most High God, met Abraham as he was returning from defeating the kings and blessed him”;
v.SAENEW: 이 멜기세덱은 살렘 왕이요, 지극히 높으신 하나님의 제사장이었습니다. 그는 아브라함이 여러 왕을 무찌르고 돌아올 때에, 그를 만나서 축복해 주었습니다.
v.WLB: 이 멜기세덱은 살렘 왕으로 지극히 높으신 하나님의 제사장입니다. 그는 왕들을 이기고 돌아오는 아브라함을 만나 축복했습니다.

v.CNV: 亚伯拉罕也把自己得来的一切，拿出十分之一来给他。麦基洗德这名字翻译出来，头一个意思就是“公义的王”；其次是“撒冷王”，就是“平安的王”的意思。
v.ESV: and to him Abraham apportioned a tenth part of everything. He is first, by translation of his name, king of righteousness, and then he is also king of Salem, that is, king of peace.
v.GAE: 아브라함이 모든 것의 십분의 일을 그에게 나누어 주니라 그 이름을 해석하면 먼저는 의의 왕이요 그 다음은 살렘 왕이니 곧 평강의 왕이요
v.KJV: To whom also Abraham gave a tenth part of all; first being by interpretation King of righteousness, and after that also King of Salem, which is, King of peace;
v.NASB: to whom also Abraham apportioned a tenth part of all the spoils, was first of all, by the translation of his name, king of righteousness, and then also king of Salem, which is king of peace.
v.NIV: and Abraham gave him a tenth of everything. First, the name Melchizedek means “king of righteousness”; then also, “king of Salem” means “king of peace.”
v.NRSV: and to him Abraham apportioned “one-tenth of everything.” His name, in the first place, means “king of righteousness”; next he is also king of Salem, that is, “king of peace.”
v.SAENEW: 아브라함은 모든 것의 십분의 일을 그에게 나누어 주었습니다. 첫째로, 멜기세덱이란 이름은 정의의 왕이라는 뜻이요, 다음으로, 그는 또한 살렘 왕인데, 그것은 평화의 왕이라는 뜻입니다.
v.WLB: 아브라함은 모든 것의 10분의 1을 그에게 주었습니다. 그의 이름의 뜻은 첫째로 ‘의의 왕’이고 다음으로 ‘살렘 왕’, 곧 ‘평화의 왕’입니다.

v.CNV: 他没有父亲，没有母亲，没有族谱，也没有生死的记录，而是与　神的儿子相似，永远作祭司。
v.ESV: He is without father or mother or genealogy, having neither beginning of days nor end of life, but resembling the Son of God he continues a priest forever.
v.GAE: 아버지도 없고 어머니도 없고 족보도 없고 시작한 날도 없고 생명의 끝도 없어 하나님의 아들과 닮아서 항상 제사장으로 있느니라
v.KJV: Without father, without mother, without descent, having neither beginning of days, nor end of life; but made like unto the Son of God; abideth a priest continually.
v.NASB: Without father, without mother, without genealogy, having neither beginning of days nor end of life, but made like the Son of God, he remains a priest perpetually.
v.NIV: Without father or mother, without genealogy, without beginning of days or end of life, resembling the Son of God, he remains a priest forever.
v.NRSV: Without father, without mother, without genealogy, having neither beginning of days nor end of life, but resembling the Son of God, he remains a priest forever.
v.SAENEW: 그에게는 아버지도 없고, 어머니도 없고, 족보도 없고, 생애의 시작도 없고, 생명의 끝도 없습니다. 그는 하나님의 아들과 같아서, 언제까지나 제사장으로 계신 분입니다.
v.WLB: 그는 아버지도 없고 어머니도 없고 족보도 없습니다. 생애의 시작도 없고 생명의 끝도 없지만 하나님의 아들을 닮아 항상 제사장으로 있습니다.

v.CNV: 你们想一想这人是多么伟大啊！祖先亚伯拉罕也要从上等的掳物中，拿出十分之一来给了他。
v.ESV: See how great this man was to whom Abraham the patriarch gave a tenth of the spoils!
v.GAE: 이 사람이 얼마나 높은가를 생각해 보라 조상 아브라함도 노략물 중 십분의 일을 그에게 주었느니라
v.KJV: Now consider how great this man was, unto whom even the patriarch Abraham gave the tenth of the spoils.
v.NASB: Now observe how great this man was to whom Abraham, the patriarch, gave a tenth of the choicest spoils.
v.NIV: Just think how great he was: Even the patriarch Abraham gave him a tenth of the plunder!
v.NRSV: See how great he is! Even Abraham the patriarch gave him a tenth of the spoils.
v.SAENEW: 멜기세덱이 얼마나 위대한가를 생각해 보십시오. 족장인 아브라함까지도 가장 좋은 전리품의 십분의 일을 그에게 바쳤습니다.
v.WLB: 그가 얼마나 위대한지 생각해 보십시오. 족장 아브라함도 그에게 10분의 1을 바쳤습니다.

v.CNV: 那些领受祭司职分的利未子孙，奉命按照律法向人民，就是自己的弟兄，收取十分之一；虽然他们都是出于亚伯拉罕的。
v.ESV: And those descendants of Levi who receive the priestly office have a commandment in the law to take tithes from the people, that is, from their brothers, though these also are descended from Abraham.
v.GAE: 레위의 아들들 가운데 제사장의 직분을 받은 자들은 율법을 따라 아브라함의 허리에서 난 자라도 자기 형제인 백성에게서 십분의 일을 취하라는 명령을 받았으나
v.KJV: And verily they that are of the sons of Levi, who receive the office of the priesthood, have a commandment to take tithes of the people according to the law, that is, of their brethren, though they come out of the loins of Abraham:
v.NASB: And those indeed of the sons of Levi who receive the priest's office have commandment in the Law to collect a tenth from the people, that is, from their brethren, although these are descended from Abraham.
v.NIV: Now the law requires the descendants of Levi who become priests to collect a tenth from the people —that is, from their fellow Israelites—even though they also are descended from Abraham.
v.NRSV: And those descendants of Levi who receive the priestly office have a commandment in the law to collect tithes from the people, that is, from their kindred, though these also are descended from Abraham.
v.SAENEW: 레위 자손 가운데서 제사장 직분을 맡는 사람들은, 자기네 동족인 이스라엘 백성에게서, 비록 그 백성도 아브라함의 자손이지만, 율법을 따라 십분의 일을 받아들이라는 명령을 받았습니다.
v.WLB: 레위 자손 가운데 제사장 직분을 받은 사람들은 자기 형제인 백성들도 아브라함의 허리에서 나왔지만 율법을 따라 그들에게서 10분의 1을 거두라는 명령을 받았습니다.

v.CNV: 可是那不与他们同谱系的麦基洗德，反而收纳了亚伯拉罕的十分之一，并且给这蒙受应许的人祝福。
v.ESV: But this man who does not have his descent from them received tithes from Abraham and blessed him who had the promises.
v.GAE: 레위 족보에 들지 아니한 멜기세덱은 아브라함에게서 십분의 일을 취하고 약속을 받은 그를 위하여 복을 빌었나니
v.KJV: But he whose descent is not counted from them received tithes of Abraham, and blessed him that had the promises.
v.NASB: But the one whose genealogy is not traced from them collected a tenth from Abraham and blessed the one who had the promises.
v.NIV: This man, however, did not trace his descent from Levi, yet he collected a tenth from Abraham and blessed him who had the promises.
v.NRSV: But this man, who does not belong to their ancestry, collected tithes from Abraham and blessed him who had received the promises.
v.SAENEW: 그러나 멜기세덱은 그들의 족보에 들지도 않았지만, 아브라함에게서 십분의 일을 받았고, 하나님의 약속을 받은 그 사람을 축복해 주었습니다.
v.WLB: 그러나 이들로부터 나오지 않은 이 멜기세덱은 아브라함에게서 10분의 1을 취했고 약속을 받은 그를 축복했습니다.

v.CNV: 向来都是位分大的给位分小的祝福，这是毫无疑问的。
v.ESV: It is beyond dispute that the inferior is blessed by the superior.
v.GAE: 논란의 여지 없이 낮은 자가 높은 자에게서 축복을 받느니라
v.KJV: And without all contradiction the less is blessed of the better.
v.NASB: But without any dispute the lesser is blessed by the greater.
v.NIV: And without doubt the lesser is blessed by the greater.
v.NRSV: It is beyond dispute that the inferior is blessed by the superior.
v.SAENEW: 두말할 것 없이, 축복은 아랫사람이 윗사람에게서 받는 법입니다.
v.WLB: 두말할 필요 없이 축복은 윗사람이 아랫사람에게 하는 것입니다.

v.CNV: 在这里，收取十分之一的，都是必死的；但在那里，收纳十分之一的，却被证实是一位活着的。
v.ESV: In the one case tithes are received by mortal men, but in the other case, by one of whom it is testified that he lives.
v.GAE: 또 여기는 죽을 자들이 십분의 일을 받으나 저기는 산다고 증거를 얻은 자가 받았느니라
v.KJV: And here men that die receive tithes; but there he receiveth them, of whom it is witnessed that he liveth.
v.NASB: In this case mortal men receive tithes, but in that case one receives them, of whom it is witnessed that he lives on.
v.NIV: In the one case, the tenth is collected by people who die; but in the other case, by him who is declared to be living.
v.NRSV: In the one case, tithes are received by those who are mortal; in the other, by one of whom it is testified that he lives.
v.SAENEW: 한 편에서는 죽을 수밖에 없는 사람들이 십분의 일을 받고, 다른 한 편에서는 살아 계시다고 입증되시는 분이 그것을 받습니다.
v.WLB: 앞의 경우는 죽게 될 사람들이 10분의 1을 받았고 뒤의 경우는 살아 있다는 증거를 받은 사람이 10분의 1을 받은 것입니다.

v.CNV: 并且可以这样说，连那收取十分之一的利未，也透过亚伯拉罕缴纳了十分之一。
v.ESV: One might even say that Levi himself, who receives tithes, paid tithes through Abraham,
v.GAE: 또한 십분의 일을 받는 레위도 아브라함으로 말미암아 십분의 일을 바쳤다고 할 수 있나니
v.KJV: And as I may so say, Levi also, who receiveth tithes, payed tithes in Abraham.
v.NASB: And, so to speak, through Abraham even Levi, who received tithes, paid tithes,
v.NIV: One might even say that Levi, who collects the tenth, paid the tenth through Abraham,
v.NRSV: One might even say that Levi himself, who receives tithes, paid tithes through Abraham,
v.SAENEW: 말하자면, 십분의 일을 받는 레위까지도 아브라함을 통해서 십분의 일을 바친 셈이 됩니다.
v.WLB: 말하자면 10분의 1을 받는 레위도 아브라함을 통해 10분의 1을 바쳤다고 할 수 있습니다.

v.CNV: 因为麦基洗德迎接亚伯拉罕的时候，利未还在他祖先的身体里面。
v.ESV: for he was still in the loins of his ancestor when Melchizedek met him.
v.GAE: 이는 멜기세덱이 아브라함을 만날 때에 레위는 이미 자기 조상의 허리에 있었음이라
v.KJV: For he was yet in the loins of his father, when Melchisedec met him.
v.NASB: for he was still in the loins of his father when Melchizedek met him.
v.NIV: because when Melchizedek met Abraham, Levi was still in the body of his ancestor.
v.NRSV: for he was still in the loins of his ancestor when Melchizedek met him.
v.SAENEW: 멜기세덱이 아브라함을 만났을 때에는, 레위는 아직 자기 조상 아브라함의 허리 속에 있었으니 말입니다.
v.WLB: 이는 멜기세덱이 아브라함을 만났을 때 레위는 아직 조상의 허리에 있었기 때문입니다.

v.CNV: 这样看来，如果借着利未人的祭司制度能达到完全的地步（人民是在这制度下领受律法的），为什么还需要照着麦基洗德的体系，另外兴起一位祭司，而不照着亚伦的体系呢？
v.ESV: Now if perfection had been attainable through the Levitical priesthood (for under it the people received the law), what further need would there have been for another priest to arise after the order of Melchizedek, rather than one named after the order of Aaron?
v.GAE: 레위 계통의 제사 직분으로 말미암아 온전함을 얻을 수 있었으면 (백성이 그 아래에서 율법을 받았으니) 어찌하여 아론의 반차를 따르지 않고 멜기세덱의 반차를 따르는 다른 한 제사장을 세울 필요가 있느냐
v.KJV: If therefore perfection were by the Levitical priesthood, (for under it the people received the law,) what further need was there that another priest should rise after the order of Melchisedec, and not be called after the order of Aaron?
v.NASB: Now if perfection was through the Levitical priesthood (for on the basis of it the people received the Law), what further need was there for another priest to arise according to the order of Melchizedek, and not be designated according to the order of Aaron?
v.NIV: If perfection could have been attained through the Levitical priesthood—and indeed the law given to the people established that priesthood—why was there still need for another priest to come, one in the order of Melchizedek, not in the order of Aaron?
v.NRSV: Now if perfection had been attainable through the levitical priesthood — for the people received the law under this priesthood — what further need would there have been to speak of another priest arising according to the order of Melchizedek, rather than one according to the order of Aaron?
v.SAENEW: 그런데 이 레위 계통의 제사직과 관련하여, 이스라엘 백성은 율법으로 지령을 받기는 하였습니다. 그러나 만일 그 제사직으로 완전한 것이 이루어질 수 있었다면, 아론의 계통이 아닌 멜기세덱의 계통을 따른 다른 제사장이 생겨날 필요가 어디에 있겠습니까?
v.WLB: 만일 레위 계열의 제사장 직분을 통해 완전함을 얻을 수 있었다면 (그런데 백성들은 이것을 근거로 율법을 받았습니다) 아론의 계열을 따르지 않고 멜기세덱의 계열을 따르는 다른 한 제사장을 세워야 할 필요가 있었겠습니까?

v.CNV: 祭司的制度既然更改了，律法也必须更改。
v.ESV: For when there is a change in the priesthood, there is necessarily a change in the law as well.
v.GAE: 제사 직분이 바꾸어졌은즉 율법도 반드시 바꾸어지리니
v.KJV: For the priesthood being changed, there is made of necessity a change also of the law.
v.NASB: For when the priesthood is changed, of necessity there takes place a change of law also.
v.NIV: For when the priesthood is changed, the law must be changed also.
v.NRSV: For when there is a change in the priesthood, there is necessarily a change in the law as well.
v.SAENEW: 제사직분에 변화가 생기면, 율법에도 반드시 변화가 생기기 마련입니다.
v.WLB: 제사장직이 변하면 율법도 반드시 변하게 됩니다.

v.CNV: 因为这些话所指的那位，原是属于另外一个支派的，这支派向来没有人在祭坛前供职。
v.ESV: For the one of whom these things are spoken belonged to another tribe, from which no one has ever served at the altar.
v.GAE: 이것은 한 사람도 제단 일을 받들지 않는 다른 지파에 속한 자를 가리켜 말한 것이라
v.KJV: For he of whom these things are spoken pertaineth to another tribe, of which no man gave attendance at the altar.
v.NASB: For the one concerning whom these things are spoken belongs to another tribe, from which no one has officiated at the altar.
v.NIV: He of whom these things are said belonged to a different tribe, and no one from that tribe has ever served at the altar.
v.NRSV: Now the one of whom these things are spoken belonged to another tribe, from which no one has ever served at the altar.
v.SAENEW: 이런 말이 가리키는 분은 레위 지파가 아닌 다른 지파에 속한 분입니다. 그 지파에 속한 사람으로서는 아무도 제단에 종사한 적이 없습니다.
v.WLB: 이것들은 그분과 관련해 언급됐습니다. 그분은 다른 지파에 속했는데 그 지파에서는 아무도 제단에서 섬긴 적이 없었습니다.

v.CNV: 我们的主明明是从犹大支派出来的，关于这个支派，摩西并没有提及祭司的事。
v.ESV: For it is evident that our Lord was descended from Judah, and in connection with that tribe Moses said nothing about priests.
v.GAE: 우리 주께서는 유다로부터 나신 것이 분명하도다 이 지파에는 모세가 제사장들에 관하여 말한 것이 하나도 없고
v.KJV: For it is evident that our Lord sprang out of Juda; of which tribe Moses spake nothing concerning priesthood.
v.NASB: For it is evident that our Lord was descended from Judah, a tribe with reference to which Moses spoke nothing concerning priests.
v.NIV: For it is clear that our Lord descended from Judah, and in regard to that tribe Moses said nothing about priests.
v.NRSV: For it is evident that our Lord was descended from Judah, and in connection with that tribe Moses said nothing about priests.
v.SAENEW: 우리 주님께서는 유다 지파에서 나신 것이 명백합니다. 그런데 모세는 제사장들에 관하여 말할 때에, 이 지파와 관련해서는 말한 것이 아무것도 없습니다.
v.WLB: 우리 주께서는 유다 지파에서 나오신 것이 분명합니다. 그런데 그 지파에 관해서는 모세가 제사장직과 관련해 아무것도 말한 적이 없습니다.

v.CNV: 如果有另一位像麦基洗德那样的祭司兴起来，那么，这里所说的就更明显了。
v.ESV: This becomes even more evident when another priest arises in the likeness of Melchizedek,
v.GAE: 멜기세덱과 같은 별다른 한 제사장이 일어난 것을 보니 더욱 분명하도다
v.KJV: And it is yet far more evident: for that after the similitude of Melchisedec there ariseth another priest,
v.NASB: And this is clearer still, if another priest arises according to the likeness of Melchizedek,
v.NIV: And what we have said is even more clear if another priest like Melchizedek appears,
v.NRSV: It is even more obvious when another priest arises, resembling Melchizedek,
v.SAENEW: 멜기세덱과 같은 모양으로 다른 제사장이 생겨난 것을 보면, 이 사실은 더욱더 명백합니다.
v.WLB: 그리고 이 사실은 멜기세덱과 흡사한 다른 한 제사장이 일어난 것을 볼 때 더욱 분명합니다.

v.CNV: 他成了祭司，不是按着律法上肉身的条例，却是按着不能毁坏的生命的大能。
v.ESV: who has become a priest, not on the basis of a legal requirement concerning bodily descent, but by the power of an indestructible life.
v.GAE: 그는 육신에 속한 한 계명의 법을 따르지 아니하고 오직 불멸의 생명의 능력을 따라 되었으니
v.KJV: Who is made, not after the law of a carnal commandment, but after the power of an endless life.
v.NASB: who has become such not on the basis of a law of physical requirement, but according to the power of an indestructible life.
v.NIV: one who has become a priest not on the basis of a regulation as to his ancestry but on the basis of the power of an indestructible life.
v.NRSV: one who has become a priest, not through a legal requirement concerning physical descent, but through the power of an indestructible life.
v.SAENEW: 그는 제사장의 혈통에 대해서 규정한 율법을 따라 제사장이 되신 것이 아니라, 썩지 않는 생명의 능력을 따라 되셨습니다.
v.WLB: 그는 육체에 속한 규례, 곧 율법을 따라 제사장이 되신 것이 아니라 썩지 않는 생명의 힘을 따라 되신 것입니다.

v.CNV: 因为有为他作证的说：“你永远作祭司，是照着麦基洗德的体系。”
v.ESV: For it is witnessed of him, “You are a priest forever, after the order of Melchizedek.”
v.GAE: 증언하기를 네가 영원히 멜기세덱의 반차를 따르는 제사장이라 하였도다
v.KJV: For he testifieth, Thou art a priest for ever after the order of Melchisedec.
v.NASB: For it is attested of Him, 'YOU ARE A PRIEST FOREVER ACCORDING TO THE ORDER OF MELCHIZEDEK.'
v.NIV: For it is declared: “You are a priest forever, in the order of Melchizedek.”
v.NRSV: For it is attested of him, “You are a priest forever, according to the order of Melchizedek.”
v.SAENEW: 그를 두고서 말하기를 "너는 멜기세덱의 계통을 따라서, 영원히 제사장이다" 한 증언이 있습니다.
v.WLB: 그렇기 때문에 “너는 영원히 멜기세덱의 계열을 따르는 제사장이다”라고 증거됐습니다.

v.CNV: 一方面，从前的条例因为软弱，没有用处，就废弃了；
v.ESV: For on the one hand, a former commandment is set aside because of its weakness and uselessness
v.GAE: 전에 있던 계명은 연약하고 무익하므로 폐하고
v.KJV: For there is verily a disannulling of the commandment going before for the weakness and unprofitableness thereof.
v.NASB: For, on the one hand, there is a setting aside of a former commandment because of its weakness and uselessness
v.NIV: The former regulation is set aside because it was weak and useless
v.NRSV: There is, on the one hand, the abrogation of an earlier commandment because it was weak and ineffectual
v.SAENEW: 전에 있던 계명은 무력하고 무익하므로 폐하게 되었습니다.
v.WLB: 전에 있던 계명은 약하고 효력이 없어 폐지됐습니다.

v.CNV: （因为律法从来没有使什么得到完全，）另一方面，它却带来了更美的盼望，借着这盼望，我们就可以亲近　神。
v.ESV: (for the law made nothing perfect); but on the other hand, a better hope is introduced, through which we draw near to God.
v.GAE: (율법은 아무 것도 온전하게 못할지라) 이에 더 좋은 소망이 생기니 이것으로 우리가 하나님께 가까이 가느니라
v.KJV: For the law made nothing perfect, but the bringing in of a better hope did; by the which we draw nigh unto God.
v.NASB: (for the Law made nothing perfect), and on the other hand there is a bringing in of a better hope, through which we draw near to God.
v.NIV: (for the law made nothing perfect), and a better hope is introduced, by which we draw near to God.
v.NRSV: (for the law made nothing perfect); there is, on the other hand, the introduction of a better hope, through which we approach God.
v.SAENEW: 율법은 아무것도 완전하게 하지 못하였습니다. 그래서 하나님께서는 더 좋은 소망을 우리에게 주셨습니다. 우리는 이 소망을 힘입어서 하나님께 가까이 나아갑니다.
v.WLB: (이는 율법이 아무것도 완전하게 할 수 없기 때문입니다.) 그러므로 더 나은 소망이 들어왔고 이를 통해 우리가 하나님께 가까이 나아가는 것입니다.

v.CNV: 此外，还有关于誓言的事。其他成为祭司的，并不是用誓言立的；
v.ESV: And it was not without an oath. For those who formerly became priests were made such without an oath,
v.GAE: 또 예수께서 제사장이 되신 것은 맹세 없이 된 것이 아니니
v.KJV: And inasmuch as not without an oath he was made priest:
v.NASB: And inasmuch as it was not without an oath
v.NIV: And it was not without an oath! Others became priests without any oath,
v.NRSV: This was confirmed with an oath; for others who became priests took their office without an oath,
v.SAENEW: 그리고 예수께서는 하나님의 맹세 없이 제사장이 되신 것이 아닙니다. 레위 계통의 사람들은 맹세 없이 제사장이 되었습니다.
v.WLB: 예수께서는 맹세 없이 제사장이 되신 것이 아닙니다. 레위 계통의 사람들은 맹세 없이 제사장이 됐습니다.

v.CNV: 只有耶稣是用誓言立的，因为那立他的对他说：“主已经起了誓，决不改变，你永远作祭司。”
v.ESV: but this one was made a priest with an oath by the one who said to him: “The Lord has sworn and will not change his mind, ‘You are a priest forever.’”
v.GAE: (그들은 맹세 없이 제사장이 되었으되 오직 예수는 자기에게 말씀하신 이로 말미암아 맹세로 되신 것이라 주께서 맹세하시고 뉘우치지 아니하시리니 네가 영원히 제사장이라 하셨도다)
v.KJV: (For those priests were made without an oath; but this with an oath by him that said unto him, The Lord sware and will not repent, Thou art a priest for ever after the order of Melchisedec:)
v.NASB: (for they indeed became priests without an oath, but He with an oath through the One who said to Him, 'THE LORD HAS SWORN AND WILL NOT CHANGE HIS MIND, 'YOU ARE A PRIEST FOREVER '');
v.NIV: but he became a priest with an oath when God said to him: “The Lord has sworn and will not change his mind: ‘You are a priest forever.’”
v.NRSV: but this one became a priest with an oath, because of the one who said to him, “The Lord has sworn and will not change his mind, ‘You are a priest forever’” —
v.SAENEW: 그러나 예수께서는 자기에게 말씀하시는 분의 맹세로 제사장이 되신 것입니다. "주님께서 맹세하셨으니, 주님은 마음을 바꾸지 않으실 것이다. 너는 영원히 제사장이다" 하셨습니다.
v.WLB: (그러나 그는 자기에게 말씀하시는 분의 맹세로 제사장이 되셨습니다. “주께서 맹세하셨으니 그분은 마음을 바꾸지 않으실 것이다. 너는 영원히 제사장이다.”)

v.CNV: 耶稣既然是用誓言立的，就成了更美好的约的保证。
v.ESV: This makes Jesus the guarantor of a better covenant.
v.GAE: 이와 같이 예수는 더 좋은 언약의 보증이 되셨느니라
v.KJV: By so much was Jesus made a surety of a better testament.
v.NASB: so much the more also Jesus has become the guarantee of a better covenant.
v.NIV: Because of this oath, Jesus has become the guarantor of a better covenant.
v.NRSV: accordingly Jesus has also become the guarantee of a better covenant.
v.SAENEW: 이렇게 해서, 예수께서는 더 좋은 언약을 보증하시는 분이 되셨습니다.
v.WLB: 이와 같이 예수께서는 더 좋은 언약을 보증해 주시는 분이 되셨습니다.

v.CNV: 一方面，从前那些作祭司的，因为受死亡的限制，不能长久留任，所以人数众多。
v.ESV: The former priests were many in number, because they were prevented by death from continuing in office,
v.GAE: 제사장 된 그들의 수효가 많은 것은 죽음으로 말미암아 항상 있지 못함이로되
v.KJV: And they truly were many priests, because they were not suffered to continue by reason of death:
v.NASB: The former priests, on the one hand, existed in greater numbers because they were prevented by death from continuing,
v.NIV: Now there have been many of those priests, since death prevented them from continuing in office;
v.NRSV: Furthermore, the former priests were many in number, because they were prevented by death from continuing in office;
v.SAENEW: 또한 레위 계통의 제사장들은 죽음 때문에 그 직무를 계속할 수 없어서, 그 수가 많아졌습니다.
v.WLB: 레위 계통의 제사장들은 죽음 때문에 그 직책을 계속 수행할 수 없기 때문에 그 수가 많았습니다.

v.CNV: 另一方面，因为耶稣是永远长存的，就拥有他永不更改的祭司职位。
v.ESV: but he holds his priesthood permanently, because he continues forever.
v.GAE: 예수는 영원히 계시므로 그 제사장 직분도 갈리지 아니하느니라
v.KJV: But this man, because he continueth ever, hath an unchangeable priesthood.
v.NASB: but Jesus, on the other hand, because He continues forever, holds His priesthood permanently.
v.NIV: but because Jesus lives forever, he has a permanent priesthood.
v.NRSV: but he holds his priesthood permanently, because he continues forever.
v.SAENEW: 그러나 예수는 영원히 계시는 분이므로, 제사장직을 영구히 간직하십니다.
v.WLB: 그러나 예수께서는 영원히 사시는 분이시므로 제사장직을 영원히 누리십니다.

v.CNV: 因此，那些靠着他进到　神面前的人，他都能拯救到底；因为他长远活着，为他们代求。
v.ESV: Consequently, he is able to save to the uttermost those who draw near to God through him, since he always lives to make intercession for them.
v.GAE: 그러므로 자기를 힘입어 하나님께 나아가는 자들을 온전히 구원하실 수 있으니 이는 그가 항상 살아 계셔서 그들을 위하여 간구하심이라
v.KJV: Wherefore he is able also to save them to the uttermost that come unto God by him, seeing he ever liveth to make intercession for them.
v.NASB: Therefore He is able also to save forever those who draw near to God through Him, since He always lives to make intercession for them.
v.NIV: Therefore he is able to save completely those who come to God through him, because he always lives to intercede for them.
v.NRSV: Consequently he is able for all time to save those who approach God through him, since he always lives to make intercession for them.
v.SAENEW: 따라서 그는 자기를 통하여 하나님께 나아오는 사람들을 완전하게 구원하실 수 있습니다. 그는 늘 살아 계셔서 그들을 위하여 중재의 간구를 하십니다.
v.WLB: 그러므로 예수께서는 자신을 통해서 하나님께 나아오는 사람들을 온전히 구원하실 수 있습니다. 그는 항상 살아 계셔서 그들을 위해 중보기도를 하십니다.

v.CNV: 这样的一位大祭司，对我们本是合适的。他是圣洁、没有邪恶、没有玷污、从罪人中分别出来、高过众天的。
v.ESV: For it was indeed fitting that we should have such a high priest, holy, innocent, unstained, separated from sinners, and exalted above the heavens.
v.GAE: 이러한 대제사장은 우리에게 합당하니 거룩하고 악이 없고 더러움이 없고 죄인에게서 떠나 계시고 하늘보다 높이 되신 이라
v.KJV: For such an high priest became us, who is holy, harmless, undefiled, separate from sinners, and made higher than the heavens;
v.NASB: For it was fitting for us to have such a high priest, holy, innocent, undefiled, separated from sinners and exalted above the heavens;
v.NIV: Such a high priest truly meets our need—one who is holy, blameless, pure, set apart from sinners, exalted above the heavens.
v.NRSV: For it was fitting that we should have such a high priest, holy, blameless, undefiled, separated from sinners, and exalted above the heavens.
v.SAENEW: 예수는 이러한 제사장으로 우리에게 적격이십니다. 그는 거룩하시고, 순진하시고, 순결하시고, 죄인들과 구별되시고, 하늘보다 높이 되신 분입니다.
v.WLB: 이러한 대제사장이야말로 우리에게 합당합니다. 그분은 거룩하고 순결하고 흠이 없고 죄인들과 구별되시며 하늘보다 높은 곳으로 오르신 분이십니다.

v.CNV: 他不必像那些大祭司，天天先为自己的罪献祭，然后为人民的罪献祭；因为他献上了自己，就把这事一次而永远的成全了。
v.ESV: He has no need, like those high priests, to offer sacrifices daily, first for his own sins and then for those of the people, since he did this once for all when he offered up himself.
v.GAE: 그는 저 대제사장들이 먼저 자기 죄를 위하고 다음에 백성의 죄를 위하여 날마다 제사 드리는 것과 같이 할 필요가 없으니 이는 그가 단번에 자기를 드려 이루셨음이라
v.KJV: Who needeth not daily, as those high priests, to offer up sacrifice, first for his own sins, and then for the people’s: for this he did once, when he offered up himself.
v.NASB: who does not need daily, like those high priests, to offer up sacrifices, first for His own sins and then for the sins of the people, because this He did once for all when He offered up Himself.
v.NIV: Unlike the other high priests, he does not need to offer sacrifices day after day, first for his own sins, and then for the sins of the people. He sacrificed for their sins once for all when he offered himself.
v.NRSV: Unlike the other high priests, he has no need to offer sacrifices day after day, first for his own sins, and then for those of the people; this he did once for all when he offered himself.
v.SAENEW: 그는 다른 대제사장들처럼 날마다 먼저 자기 죄를 위하여 희생제물을 드리고, 그 다음에 백성을 위하여 희생제물을 드릴 필요가 없습니다. 그는 자기 자신을 바치셔서 단 한 번에 이 일을 이루셨기 때문입니다.
v.WLB: 그분은 다른 제사장들처럼 먼저 자신의 죄를 위해, 그 다음에 백성들의 죄를 위해 날마다 제사를 드릴 필요가 없습니다. 이는 그분이 자신을 드려 단번에 이 일을 이루셨기 때문입니다.

v.CNV: 律法所立的大祭司，都是软弱的人；可是在律法以后，用誓言所立的儿子，却是成为完全直到永远的。
v.ESV: For the law appoints men in their weakness as high priests, but the word of the oath, which came later than the law, appoints a Son who has been made perfect forever.
v.GAE: 율법은 약점을 가진 사람들을 제사장으로 세웠거니와 율법 후에 하신 맹세의 말씀은 영원히 온전하게 되신 아들을 세우셨느니라
v.KJV: For the law maketh men high priests which have infirmity; but the word of the oath, which was since the law, maketh the Son, who is consecrated for evermore.
v.NASB: For the Law appoints men as high priests who are weak, but the word of the oath, which came after the Law, appoints a Son, made perfect forever.
v.NIV: For the law appoints as high priests men in all their weakness; but the oath, which came after the law, appointed the Son, who has been made perfect forever.
v.NRSV: For the law appoints as high priests those who are subject to weakness, but the word of the oath, which came later than the law, appoints a Son who has been made perfect forever.
v.SAENEW: 사람들에게 약점이 있어도 율법은 어쩔 수 없이 그들을 대제사장으로 세우지만, 율법이 생긴 이후에 하나님께서 맹세하신 말씀은 영원히 완전하게 되신 아들을 대제사장으로 세웠습니다.
v.WLB: 율법은 연약함을 가진 사람을 대제사장으로 세웠으나 율법 후에 주어진 맹세의 말씀은 영원히 완전케 되신 아들을 대제사장으로 세웠습니다.
